tweets.text: This brings its total Bitcoin holdings to 847,363 BTC, with cumulative purchases reaching $64.1 billion and an average cost of $75,651 per Bitcoin.

tweets.author: zonecrypto.bsky.social
tweets.text: As trading commenced on Wall Street Monday, Bitcoin (BTC) crossed the $65,000 mark, influenced by the liquidity in exchange order books. Notably, Bitcoin reached a new high for the week, even as US stocks began to falter.

tweets.author: zonecrypto.bsky.social
tweets.text: ✦ Bitcoin price rises to $65.5K as Iran agreement impacts oil prices significantly: Bitcoin is aiming for a breakout with a target near $70,000, buoyed by recent developments in Iran leading to a decline in oil prices to their lowest since early March.

tweets.author: zonecrypto.bsky.social
tweets.text: Additionally, Bitcoin futures open interest has decreased by 19.5% since June, funding rates have dropped to 0.02%, and ETF outflows have significantly reduced to $540m from $5.5B last month, indicating reduced selling pressure.

tweets.author: zonecrypto.bsky.social
tweets.text: ✦ Bitcoin shows potential market bottom with weekly close above $63K: Bitcoin has maintained a weekly close above $63,000 for three consecutive weeks, suggesting a potential market bottom following a dip to a 2026 low around $59,000.
